C1_LR_GOVERNANCE_R2.allowed_wording: For LR, blind MI removal improves directional repair (+0.085 [+0.070,+0.098]) and leak recall (+0.403 [+0.375,+0.430]) over matched random removal, but also increases overcorrection (+0.041 [+0.018,+0.068]).
C1_LR_GOVERNANCE_R2.forbidden_wording: MI removal unambiguously repairs tabular leakage.

C1_RF_GOVERNANCE_R2.allowed_wording: For RF, blind MI removal improves directional repair (+0.096 [+0.086,+0.107]) and leak recall (+0.403 [+0.375,+0.430]) over matched random removal, but also increases overcorrection (+0.042 [+0.021,+0.066]).
C1_RF_GOVERNANCE_R2.forbidden_wording: MI removal unambiguously repairs tabular leakage.

C1_LightGBM_GOVERNANCE_R2.allowed_wording: For LightGBM, blind MI removal improves directional repair (+0.101 [+0.090,+0.111]) and leak recall (+0.403 [+0.375,+0.430]) over matched random removal, but also increases overcorrection (+0.045 [+0.023,+0.070]).
C1_LightGBM_GOVERNANCE_R2.forbidden_wording: MI removal unambiguously repairs tabular leakage.

C2_NO_LEARNER_INTERACTION_R2.allowed_wording: The governance advantage pattern (positive directional repair + leak recall, with overcorrection caveat) is consistent across LR, RF, and LightGBM.
C2_NO_LEARNER_INTERACTION_R2.forbidden_wording: Learner differences are large enough to change the qualitative conclusion.
C2_NO_LEARNER_INTERACTION_R2.note: The mask-grounded metrics (Δleak_recall, Δdeletion_precision, Δlegit_retention) are identical across learners by construction (same selections). Only score-based metrics differ.

C3_OVERCORRECTION_CAVEAT_R2.allowed_wording: MI-guided removal produces more overcorrection (governed score crossing the strict reference) than random removal across all three learners: LR +0.041 [0.018,0.068], RF +0.042 [0.021,0.066], LightGBM +0.045 [0.023,0.070]. Approximately 30% of keys showing positive legacy SDR also exhibit overcorrection.
C3_OVERCORRECTION_CAVEAT_R2.forbidden_wording: MI removal is a pure repair operation without side effects.

C4_LEGACY_SDR_OVERSTATEMENT_R2.allowed_wording: The legacy SDR metric (|full-strict| - |governed-strict|) conflates directional repair with overcorrection. When decomposed by direction: directional repair shows a larger governance advantage (Δ=+0.085 for LR vs Δlegacy_sdr=+0.043), but the gain is partially offset by increased overcorrection (Δ=+0.041). The legacy SDR understates the actual repair but also masks the overcorrection cost.
C4_LEGACY_SDR_OVERSTATEMENT_R2.forbidden_wording: Legacy SDR is a complete measure of repair quality.

C6_SPARSE_ARCHETYPE_REVISED_R2.allowed_wording: Under the properly integrated multi-seed P2 protocol (20 seeds, averaged before P3 comparison), the sparse archetype shows no reliable governance advantage or disadvantage (mean Δlegacy_sdr=+0.022, CI[-0.049,+0.072]). The previously reported negative value (-0.118) was an artifact of relying on a single frozen P2 governance seed.
C6_SPARSE_ARCHETYPE_REVISED_R2.forbidden_wording: The sparse archetype is a consistently negative regime for MI-guided governance.
C6_SPARSE_ARCHETYPE_REVISED_R2.note: This changes the LOAO-sparse interpretation: when sparse is excluded, the overall remains positive because sparse was near-zero, not because it was strongly negative.

C7_M09_SEMANTIC_POSITIVE_R2.allowed_wording: M09 remains a robust positive counterexample within the structured family under R2 metrics.
C7_M09_SEMANTIC_POSITIVE_R2.forbidden_wording: M09 is negative when properly evaluated.

global_limitations: Legacy SDR conflation: the original metric cannot separate directional repair from overcorrection.
Overcorrection is systematically higher for MI-guided removal than random at 20% budget.
The sparse archetype negative finding under single-seed P2 does not replicate under multi-seed averaging.
The prior C4 claim ("sparse archetype is a negative regime") requires revision.
Mask-grounded metrics are identical across learners by construction — only score-based metrics differ.
The directional_repair_fraction metric has heavy-tailed distributions with extreme negative outliers in low-opportunity keys; this metric should NOT be used for aggregate comparisons.
